sections.paragraph: m. cor, a measure, both of dry and liquid things, 1Ki 5:2 Eze 45:14, containing ten ephahs or baths, i.q. חֹמֶר. (The Arameans commonly use כֹּור, ܟܘܽܪܳܐ for the Hebrew חֹמֶר. The Hellenists also adopted κόρος.) The origin of this word is in its round form, and כֹּר appears properly to signify a round vessel. See the root כָּרַר .
